props.text: Every Dependency Is a Handshake You Can't Take Back
The May 2026 npm supply chain attack compromised 170+ trusted packages and put 15 million users in the blast radius — not through fake packages, but by exploiting CI/CD infrastructure itself.

This article uses that event as a lens to examine MiroShark's 30-PR zero-new-deps streak: why building 13 share surfaces with nothing but Python's standard library is not minimalism for its own sake, but a structural argument about composability, trust, and software that outlasts its ecosystem.

props.title: Every Dependency Is a Handshake You Can't Take Back
